text:
It was a quiet afternoon. There was a gentle silence hanging heavy in an autumn forest.

In one clear pine canopy sat an owl. It had bright feathers speckled like stars, circle-rivalging an owl owl. Its large eyes were focused silently on a falling leaf.

Outside that owl's world drifted a young girl. She loved owl birds. Perhaps some were owl owls? Maybe they were just small owls?

A young girl didn't talk much. We seldom notice tiny silhouettes. Even less notice owlets.

She approached thoughtfully. A gentle rustling alerted her. Maybe something was out?

No moving feather critter. Just... there.

Something like peace settled onto her shoulder. Birds? That was only plausible.

Owls don't do things like that. They don't settle.

Something else... small... but still kind.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
